assertions.justification: The response explicitly labels the request as '边界不清晰 (Boundaries unclear)' and frames the problem as needing clarification before any solution is proposed.
Exactly one focused question is asked (delivery channel); the secondary note about user preference control is framed as an aside ('顺带'), not an additional question in a checklist.
The response surfaces delivery channel as the primary blind spot and explicitly calls out user notification preference control as a second assumption that would affect system complexity.
No architecture diagrams, database schemas, or implementation code appear anywhere in the response; it stops entirely at clarifying questions.
The response immediately pivots to a full solution framework ('帮你梳理思路和实现方案') without diagnosing or labeling the request as ambiguous.
Section one alone lists four clarifying sub-questions as a checklist, and the closing paragraph adds three more questions, far exceeding one focused question.
Delivery channel, trigger scenarios, real-time requirements, and user scale are all surfaced as considerations in section one.
The response includes a full architecture diagram, database DDL, Node.js service code, SSE implementation, and a phased rollout plan before any goals are clarified.

summary: The skill-guided response scored a perfect 1.0 by correctly diagnosing ambiguity and asking a single focused question, while the baseline scored 0.25 by immediately producing full architecture and implementation details without clarifying the problem.
